question: What are you looking for today?

options.text: I don't know where to start

question: Great — it's early and that's an advantage. What subjects do you enjoy most right now?

options.text: I enjoy all equally / not sure

question: You're at a crucial stage. Have you chosen your stream for Class 11 yet?
PCM opens many doors. What excites you more?

options.text: Understanding how things work — physics, space, research
Design and creativity with a technical edge

question: Arts and Humanities path. What pulls you more?
PCB opens medical and life science paths. What interests you more?
Commerce path. Where do you see yourself?
No worries — let's figure it out together. Which of these sounds most like you?

options.text: I like solving problems and puzzles
I like creating things — art, writing, music
I like working with and helping people
I like understanding how the world works

question: What are you looking for in a college?

options.text: Top ranking government college — low fees

question: No problem at all. Let's start from scratch. How old are you?
What happened during the gap year?
Engineering & Technology
title: Engineering & Technology
description: Your interest in building and design is a strong fit for engineering and technical careers.
This path leverages PCM strength and gives you practical, high-growth roles across multiple industries.
You can combine creativity with math, physics, and computing to solve real-world problems.

careers.name: Software Engineer
careers.description: Builds applications, platforms, and user-facing systems.

careers.name: Mechanical Engineer
careers.description: Designs machines, vehicles, and industrial equipment.

careers.name: Civil Engineer
careers.description: Plans and supervises construction of buildings and infrastructure.

careers.name: Architect
careers.description: Creates functional and beautiful built environments.

careers.name: Electrical Engineer
careers.description: Works on power systems, electronics and automation solutions.

exams.name: JEE Main
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to NITs, IIITs, and other engineering colleges

exams.name: JEE Advanced
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to IITs and select engineering institutes

exams.name: NATA
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to architecture colleges like CEPT and SPA

next_steps: Start solving JEE Main level physics and maths problems for 30 minutes daily.
Explore basic CAD or design software to understand architectural and engineering workflows.
Join a weekend coding or robotics workshop to build hands-on design confidence.
Track college cutoffs for NITs, IIITs, and top architecture programs this year.
timeline: Year 1: Build strong PCM fundamentals and take mock JEE/Main tests. Year 2: Focus on board exams, refine engineering interests, and apply to top institutes.

question: Physics and Research Science
title: Physics and Research Science
description: You are drawn to deep understanding and exploration, which suits research-focused science careers.
This path builds on strong fundamentals in physics, maths, and critical thinking.
It is ideal for students who want to work in space science, labs, or national research institutes.

careers.name: Research Scientist
careers.description: Conducts advanced experiments and publishes scientific findings.

careers.name: Space Scientist
careers.description: Designs and analyzes missions for space agencies like ISRO.

careers.name: Data Analyst
careers.description: Interprets scientific data and builds predictive models.

careers.name: Laboratory Scientist
careers.description: Manages laboratory research and testing workflows.

careers.name: Science Communicator
careers.description: Explains research through writing, media, or public engagement.

exams.name: IISER Aptitude Test
exams.full_form: Indian Institutes of Science Education and Research Aptitude Test

exams.name: JEE Advanced
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to IITs and research-oriented institutes

next_steps: Read popular science books on physics and space to strengthen your curiosity.
Solve advanced physics problems and practice numerical reasoning every week.
Participate in science fairs or research internships to gain practical exposure.
Watch lectures from IIT and IISER faculty to understand research career paths.
timeline: Year 1: Sharpen maths and physics foundations with advanced problems. Year 2: Prepare for KVPY/IISER and JEE Advanced, while applying for research internships.

question: Computer Science & Data Science
title: Computer Science & Data Science
description: You enjoy working with computers and patterns, a strong sign for CS and data careers.
This pathway blends technical programming with analytics and applied math.
It is excellent for students who want high growth, creative problem solving, and industry-ready skillsets.

careers.name: Data Scientist
careers.description: Builds models that turn data into business insight.

careers.name: Software Developer
careers.description: Develops software applications and systems for users.

careers.name: Machine Learning Engineer
careers.description: Implements AI models for real-world products.

careers.name: Product Analyst
careers.description: Uses data to shape product decisions and growth.

careers.name: Cloud Architect
careers.description: Designs cloud services and scalable systems.

exams.name: JEE Main
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to engineering colleges with CS branches

exams.name: BITSAT
exams.full_form: Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to BITS campuses for CS/Data programs

next_steps: Start learning Python and complete a small data or web project.
Practice logic and algorithms for 30 minutes every day.
Join an online data science challenge or hackathon to build a portfolio.
Research top CS programs at IITs, BITS, and IIITs and note their cutoffs.
timeline: Year 1: Learn coding, data basics, and solve programming problems. Year 2: Focus on JEE/engineering entrance prep and create a portfolio of small projects.

question: Architecture & Product Design
title: Architecture & Product Design
description: Your mix of technical curiosity and creativity fits architecture and design well.
This path combines spatial thinking with aesthetics and practical engineering.
It prepares you for hands-on work in buildings, products, and user-focused design.

careers.name: Architect
careers.description: Designs functional, beautiful buildings and structures.

careers.name: Product Designer
careers.description: Creates digital and physical products with user experience focus.

careers.name: Interior Designer
careers.description: Plans attractive, functional interior spaces.

careers.name: Landscape Architect
careers.description: Designs outdoor environments and public spaces.

careers.name: Design Researcher
careers.description: Studies user needs to inform product design decisions.

exams.name: JEE Paper 2
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to B.Arch programs in NITs and centrally funded colleges

exams.name: UCEED
exams.full_form: Undergraduate Common Entrance Exam for Design
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to design programs at IITs and other institutes

next_steps: Sketch designs, build simple models, and study architecture portfolios.
Visit local buildings or museums to observe form, function, and space.
Learn basic drafting or CAD tools like SketchUp or AutoCAD.
Review past NATA and UCEED question papers to understand exam patterns.
timeline: Year 1: Build a portfolio of sketches and learn design software. Year 2: Prepare for NATA/UCEED and apply to architecture/design institutes.

question: Psychology & Social Change
title: Psychology & Social Change
description: You are motivated by helping others and creating social impact.
This path suits students who care about people, community work, and social justice.
You can build a meaningful career in counseling, research, or NGO leadership.

careers.name: Clinical Psychologist
careers.description: Works with clients on mental health and therapy.

careers.name: Social Worker
careers.description: Designs and runs community support programs.

careers.name: Counselor
careers.description: Provides guidance for education, career, and emotional health.

careers.name: NGO Program Manager
careers.description: Leads projects that address social issues.

careers.name: Human Resources Specialist
careers.description: Supports employee wellbeing and organizational culture.

exams.name: CUET UG
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to psychology and social science programs

exams.name: IPU CET
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to psychology and social work courses

exams.name: NLSIU Admission Test
exams.eligibility: Graduation for social science research programs
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to specialized social science or policy courses

next_steps: Volunteer with a local NGO or helpline to understand social issues firsthand.
Take an introductory psychology or sociology course online.
Read case studies on counseling and community development work.
Meet a counselor or social worker to learn day-to-day career realities.
timeline: Year 1: Build empathy and social awareness through activities and coursework. Year 2: Apply to psychology or social science programs and pursue practical experience.

question: Law & Legal Studies
title: Law & Legal Studies
description: Your interest in justice and rules fits a career in law and tribunals.
This path works well if you enjoy debate, research, and structured argument.
A law degree opens multiple options including litigation, corporate, and policy work.

careers.name: Litigation Lawyer
careers.description: Represents clients in courts and legal disputes.

careers.name: Corporate Lawyer
careers.description: Advises companies on contracts and compliance.

careers.name: Legal Researcher
careers.description: Produces legal analysis for policy and academics.

careers.name: Judicial Clerk
careers.description: Assists judges with case preparation and legal research.

careers.name: Public Policy Advisor
careers.description: Shapes law and regulation through policy work.

exams.name: CLAT
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to NLUs and law programs

exams.name: LSAT India
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to law colleges across India

next_steps: Read about landmark court cases and legal reasoning.
Join a debate club or moot court activity.
Practice CLAT sample papers and legal aptitude questions.
Visit a law college campus or talk with a practicing lawyer.
timeline: Year 1: Build reading, reasoning and current affairs habits. Year 2: Prepare for CLAT/AILET and begin law college applications.

question: Civil Services & Public Policy
title: Civil Services & Public Policy
description: You are motivated by leadership, governance, and public service.
UPSC is a long-term path requiring broad knowledge and disciplined study.
This route suits students who want to shape society through administration.

careers.name: IAS Officer
careers.description: Leads administration and district governance.

careers.name: IPS Officer
careers.description: Leads policing and internal security operations.

careers.name: IFS Officer
careers.description: Represents India in foreign service.

careers.name: Policy Analyst
careers.description: Advises on government and development policy.

careers.name: Development Manager
careers.description: Runs public or NGO development programs.

exams.name: UPSC CSE
exams.full_form: Union Public Service Commission Civil Services Examination
exams.what_it_leads_to: Selection to IAS, IPS, IFS and other civil services

exams.name: SSC CGL
exams.full_form: Staff Selection Commission Combined Graduate Level
exams.what_it_leads_to: Central government group B and C posts

next_steps: Start reading daily newspapers and build a current affairs notebook.
Practice essay writing and answer structuring regularly.
Study Indian polity, history and economy basics in a structured way.
Take a short online foundation course for civil services exam strategy.
timeline: Year 1: Build strong general knowledge and writing habits. Year 2: Join a UPSC foundation program and start prelims/ mains preparation.

question: Media & Journalism
title: Media & Journalism
description: You are drawn to storytelling and communicating ideas to an audience.
This path fits students who love writing, media, and current affairs.
It leads to careers in journalism, content, and media production.

careers.name: Journalist

careers.name: PR Specialist

next_steps: Write opinion pieces or start a blog on topics you care about.
Record short videos or podcasts to practice storytelling.

Practice interview communication and media analysis.

question: Top Medical Colleges via NEET

Practice quantitative aptitude and logical reasoning questions.
Explore management institutes and their MBA specializations.
Consider undergraduate business programs if you are in Class 12.
timeline: Year 1: Strengthen business fundamentals and analytical skills. Year 2: Prepare for MBA entrance paths or apply to undergraduate management courses.

question: Banking Exam Path
title: Banking Exam Path
description: You are interested in stable banking careers with structured growth.
This path suits students who value disciplined government jobs.
It prepares you for bank officer roles and financial services.

careers.name: Bank PO
careers.description: Handles banking services and customer operations.

careers.name: SBI PO
careers.description: Works as a probationary officer at SBI.

careers.name: Credit Analyst
careers.description: Evaluates loan proposals and credit risk.

careers.name: Branch Manager
careers.description: Leads a bank branch and operations.

careers.name: Compliance Officer
careers.description: Ensures regulatory adherence in banking.

exams.name: IBPS PO
exams.full_form: Institute of Banking Personnel Selection Probationary Officer
exams.what_it_leads_to: PO jobs in public sector banks

exams.name: SBI PO
exams.full_form: State Bank of India Probationary Officer

exams.name: SSC CGL
exams.full_form: Staff Selection Commission Combined Graduate Level

next_steps: Practice quantitative aptitude and reasoning sections daily.
Read current affairs and banking news updates.
Make a mock-test schedule for IBPS and SBI exams.
Review past year papers and exam pattern carefully.
timeline: Year 1: Build aptitude skills and general awareness. Year 2: Take banking entrance mocks and apply to multiple exams.

question: Law School via CLAT
title: Law School via CLAT
description: You are ready for a law career through national level law schools.
This path suits students who enjoy reasoning and legal thinking.
CLAT gives access to high-quality law programs and training.

careers.name: Lawyer
careers.description: Represents clients in legal matters.

careers.name: Legal Advisor
careers.description: Offers legal guidance to organizations.

careers.name: Judicial Clerk
careers.description: Assists judges with legal research.

careers.name: Policy Analyst
careers.description: Analyzes legislation and policy impact.

careers.name: Corporate Counsel
careers.description: Handles legal issues within corporations.

exams.name: CLAT
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to NLUs and other law colleges

exams.name: LSAT India
exams.what_it_leads_to: Admission to a variety of law schools

next_steps: Practice CLAT logical reasoning and English comprehension questions.
Read opinion pieces on law and current affairs.
Attend a law entrance preparation workshop if available.
Start building a strong vocabulary and reading habit.
timeline: Year 1: Strengthen reasoning and legal reading skills. Year 2: Intensive CLAT practice and college applications.

question: Central University Arts & Social Science

Research central university humanities programs and placements.
Attend open house events or college info sessions.

question: Union Public Service Commission
title: Union Public Service Commission
description: You are interested in administration and leadership, making UPSC a strong fit.
This path demands broad knowledge, discipline and long-term motivation.
It leads to prestigious civil service careers with societal impact.

careers.name: IAS Officer
careers.description: Leads administration and governance at district/state levels.

careers.name: IPS Officer
careers.description: Leads policing and public safety initiatives.

careers.name: IFS Officer
careers.description: Represents India in foreign relations.

careers.name: Policy Analyst
careers.description: Advises on government policy and programs.

careers.name: Development Manager
careers.description: Runs public welfare and development schemes.

exams.name: UPSC CSE

exams.what_it_leads_to: Selection to IAS, IPS, IFS and other services

exams.full_form: Staff Selection Commission Combined Graduate Level

next_steps: Build a daily current affairs routine and note key issues.
Practice essay writing and answer structuring weekly.
Study polity, economy and general science in depth.
Take a UPSC foundation course if you need guided support.
timeline: Year 1: Develop general knowledge and writing abilities. Year 2: Begin UPSC prelims and mains preparation while gaining clarity on service choices.

question: Law Entrance via CLAT/AILET

question: Design & Creative Arts Entrance

question: Journalism & Mass Communication
title: Journalism & Mass Communication
description: You are passionate about media, storytelling and communication.
This path suits students who love writing, reporting and content creation.
It leads to careers in journalism, broadcasting and digital media.

Read and watch news critically to understand media styles.
Write sample news reports and feature stories each week.
